title: INISTATE — Initial State Module Schema
description: Subset of the FACTS schema for defining module structure without listings. All identifiers use display names — internal IDs, GUIDs, and numeric enums are resolved automatically by the MCP layer.

FieldType.description: Human-readable field type. The MCP layer maps these to/from internal numeric enums automatically.

FieldDefinition.description: A module field as seen through the MCP abstraction.

id.description: Stable opaque identifier. Absent on create, present on read/update. Enables renaming — the MCP layer matches by id when present, falling back to name for new fields.

name.description: Display name. Used as the key in all MCP input/output payloads (e.g. 'Leave Type', not 'xKfLmNpQ').

options.description: Available option names for Selection/Tag fields. Shorthand: Selection(Option1/Option2/Option3).

fields.description: Sub-field definitions for Table type. Each sub-field defines a column in the table.

ai_hint.description: Natural-language guidance for AI agents on how to interpret or populate this field.

id.description: Stable opaque identifier. Absent on create, present on read/update.

StateDefinition.description: A workflow state. MCP resolves names to internal GUIDs automatically.

id.description: Stable opaque identifier. Absent on create, present on read/update. Enables renaming states without breaking flows.

name.description: State display name (e.g. 'Approved', not a GUID).

color.description: Hex color code for the state badge (white text on colored background). Choose based on attention level and sentiment: #5A6070 (grey) = not started/idle, #2968A8 (blue) = waiting for action, #2A7B50 (green) = active work in progress, #A07828 (amber) = at risk/deadline approaching, #C0392B (red) = urgent/SLA breached, #6B4D91 (purple) = externally blocked, #1E6B45 (dark green) = terminal success, #8B2D2D (dark red) = terminal failure/rejected. Rules: terminal success states must use #1E6B45, terminal failure/rejection/cancellation must use #8B2D2D, only use #2A7B50 for states where work is actively being executed, use #2968A8 for approved/completed states still awaiting next action.

initial.description: When true, new entries default to this state. Only one state should be initial.

ai_hint.description: Guidance for AI agents on when an entry should be in this state.

ActivityDefinition.description: A custom activity (e.g. Approve, Reject). Standard activities (create, edit, delete, etc.) are implicit.

id.description: Stable opaque identifier. Absent on create, present on read/update. Enables renaming activities without breaking flows.

name.description: Activity display name. MCP resolves to internal activity ID.

fields.description: Fields included in this activity's form. Each item is either a field name string (shorthand) or an ActivityFieldRef object with constraints.

oneOf.description: Field display name (shorthand — defaults to required=false, readOnly=false).

ai_hint.description: Guidance for AI agents on when/how to perform this activity.

actor.description: Who is expected to perform this activity. 'human' = only a person can execute it, 'ai' = an AI agent performs it autonomously, 'hybrid' = either a person or AI agent may perform it. Defaults to 'human' when omitted.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum AI confidence required for this activity to proceed with a state transition. If ai_confidence < confidence_threshold, the state change is suppressed and the entry is flagged for human intervention. Omit or set to 0 to disable the gate.

ActivityFieldRef.description: A field reference within an activity form, with per-field constraints.

required.description: When true, this field must be filled to submit the activity.

readOnly.description: When true, the field is visible but not editable in this activity.

options.description: Subset of allowed options for Selection/Tag fields in this activity. Omit to allow all options.

FlowDefinition.description: A state transition rule. All references use display names — MCP resolves to internal IDs.

from.description: Source state name (matches StateDefinition.name). Empty string = any state.

activity.description: Activity name that triggers this transition (matches ActivityDefinition.name).

ModuleSchema.description: INISTATE module definition — the MCP-facing schema for creating and updating modules without listings. All internal complexity (GUIDs, numeric enums, design rows) is hidden. A module can be either a workflow module (with states, activities, and flows) or a record list module (master data, lookup tables, registries) that simply stores entries without any workflow. Record list modules omit states, activities, and flows entirely — entries are created, edited, and deleted using the standard activities without state transitions.

moduleId.description: Module identifier (numeric ID or name). Required for update operations to identify which module to update. Absent on create.

name.description: Module name. On create, this is the new module's name. On update, this can rename the module.

information.description: Field definitions. Order determines default display order.

states.description: Workflow states. First state with initial=true (or first state if none specified) is the default. Omit for record list modules that do not need workflow.

activities.description: Custom activities (e.g. Approve, Reject). Standard activities (create, edit, delete, view, assign, comment, etc.) are always available. Omit for record list modules that only need standard activities.

flows.description: State transition rules linking activities to state changes. Omit for record list modules with no workflow.

FileUploadResult.description: Response from uploading a file via MCP.

url.description: Relative file path in /s/{shortId}/{fileName} format. To download, prepend /api/mcp/download/{moduleName} when the URL starts with /s/.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the uploaded file.

FileFieldValue.description: Value shape for File/Image fields as stored and returned by MCP.

path.description: Relative download path in /s/{shortId}/{fileName} format. To download, prepend /api/mcp/download/{moduleName}.

FileFieldInput.description: Input shape for File/Image fields when submitting an activity. Two modes: (1) base64 upload — include 'bytes' and the server uploads to S3, or (2) pre-uploaded — include 'path' from a prior upload_file() call.

bytes.description: Base64-encoded file content. If present, the server uploads to S3 and replaces with 'path'. Omit when using a pre-uploaded path.

path.description: Pre-uploaded file path from upload_file() (e.g. '/s/xK8m/report.pdf'). Use this instead of bytes for files already uploaded via POST /api/mcp/upload.

_notes: For File/Image (single): value is one FileFieldInput object
For Files/Images (multiple): value is an array of FileFieldInput objects
After server processing, 'bytes' is removed and 'path' is added — the stored/returned shape is always FileFieldValue

EntryData.description: Entry field values as returned by MCP. Keys are field display names, values are typed according to FieldType. For File/Image fields, values are FileFieldValue objects ({ name, path }). For Files/Images fields, values are arrays of FileFieldValue objects.

Entry.description: A single module entry as seen through MCP. All field values use display names as keys.

date.description: Entry date (business date, distinct from audit timestamps).

data.description: Field values keyed by display name.

availableActivities.description: Activities available on this entry based on current state and user authorization.

ActivityForm.description: Form definition returned when preparing to perform an activity.

entryId.description: Entry ID when editing an existing entry. Null for create.

documentId.description: Document ID of the entry, if applicable.

form.description: Form fields. Each item is an object keyed by field display name, containing field definition properties.

items.description: Single field: { "Field Name": { type, required?, readOnly?, options?, module?, fields? } }

module.description: Referenced module name for Module/User field types.

fields.description: Sub-fields for Table/List types. Same keyed-by-name structure.

defaults.description: Current/default values for pre-populating the form, keyed by display name.

states.description: Available target states for this activity.

confidence_threshold.description: The activity's confidence threshold for AI-driven state transitions. Present only when the activity has a threshold configured (> 0). AI agents should compare their confidence against this value before submitting.

availableActivities.description: Activities available on the entry in its current state.

ActivitySubmission.description: Payload for performing an activity on an entry. All keys use display names.

activity.description: Activity name: create, edit, delete, or any custom activity name.

entryId.description: Required for edit/delete/custom activities. Omit for create.

entryIds.description: Multiple entry IDs for bulk operations.

input.description: Field values keyed by display name. For File/Image fields, use FileFieldInput objects ({ name, bytes } for base64 upload or { name, path } for pre-uploaded). For Files/Images fields, use arrays of FileFieldInput objects.

state.description: Target state name (resolved to internal ID automatically).

ai.description: AI agent traceability context. Include when the submission is made by an AI agent.

reasoning.description: Natural language explanation of the AI's decision.

reference.description: Identifier or location of the source.

sources.description: What data the AI used and from where.

model.description: Which model made this decision (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-20250514).

prompt_hash.description: Hash of the system prompt used, for version tracking.

confidence.description: Confidence score (0–1). If below the activity's confidence_threshold, the state transition is suppressed and the entry is flagged for human intervention.

AvailableActivities.description: Activities the current user can perform on the entry, based on authorization and current state.

standard.description: Standard activities available (e.g. create, edit, delete, changeState, duplicate, comment, manage).

custom.description: Custom activities available based on current state and flows.

stateFlow.description: Current state and possible transitions. Present only when the module has states.

currentState.description: Current state name of the entry.

transitions.description: Per-activity target state names. Keys are activity names, values are arrays of possible target state names.

flagged.description: True when AI confidence was below the activity's confidence_threshold. State transition was suppressed and the entry is flagged for human intervention (collaboration flags: WithIntention + LastIntention, history type: intention). Only present when true.

availableActivities.description: Activities available on the entry after this activity was performed.

ai.description: AI agent traceability context captured at submission time. Present only on events submitted by an AI agent.

reasoning.description: Natural language explanation of the AI's decision.

EntryHistory.description: Paginated audit trail for an entry.

FilterOperators.description: Available filter operators for list_entries. Keys are field display names. Simple value = equality match.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error shape returned by all MCP endpoints.

operations._description: INISTATE operations map to MCP endpoints. Each operation uses display names exclusively — the MCP layer handles all internal ID resolution.

discover_modules.description: List all available modules in the workspace.

get_module.description: Get module definition including fields, states, and activities.

tier.description: basic = fields + states only. extended = + activities, flows.

list_entries.description: Query entries with filters, pagination, and sorting.

filters.description: Field display names as keys. Simple value = equality. Object value = operator(s).

sortBy.description: Field display name to sort by.

get_entry.description: Read a single entry by ID.

get_form.description: Get the form definition for an activity, including required fields, types, options, and current values.

submit_activity.description: Perform an activity: create, edit, delete, or any custom activity.

get_history.description: Get audit trail and comments for an entry.

upload_file.description: Upload a file to S3. Returns a /s/ URL that can be used as a File/Image field value in submit_activity. To download, the agent prepends /api/mcp/download/{moduleName} to the returned URL.

module.description: Module name (optional, for scoping the file to a module).

_notes: Request must be multipart/form-data with the file as a form part
Optional 'module' form field scopes the file to a module's S3 folder
Blocked extensions: .exe, .bat, .cmd, .dll, .msi, and other executable types
Filenames are sanitized (path traversal characters stripped)

download_file.description: Download a file by module name. AI agents construct this URL by prepending /api/mcp/download/{moduleName} to a /s/{shortId}/{fileName} URL.

guid.description: Short ID from the file URL.

returns.description: Redirects to a pre-signed S3 URL (1hr TTL) or streams the file directly for Office applications.

_notes: Returns 404 with MCP error format if file not found in S3
Office user-agents receive direct stream; all others get a 302 redirect to a pre-signed URL

get_module_schema.description: Get an existing module definition in full INISTATE ModuleSchema format with stable IDs. The output is round-trippable — it can be modified and sent back via update_module.

create_module.description: Create a new module from an INISTATE ModuleSchema definition.

update_module.description: Update an existing module. The moduleId identifies which module to update; name can be changed to rename. Omitted sections are left unchanged.

workflow_guide._description: Recommended sequence for AI agents interacting through INISTATE.
steps: 2. get_module(module, tier='basic') → understand fields and states
3. get_module(module, tier='extended') → understand activities (only when needed)
4. get_form(module, activity) → get required fields, types, options, defaults, and confidence_threshold before submitting
5. upload_file(module, file) → upload files for File/Image fields, returns /s/ URL
6. submit_activity(module, activity, input) → create/edit/delete/custom operations. Use /s/ URLs from upload_file for File/Image fields.
7. list_entries(module, filters) → query and browse entries
8. get_entry(module, entryId) → read a specific entry
9. get_history(module, entryId) → audit trail and comments
10. download_file(moduleName, guid, fileName) → download a file. Construct URL: /api/mcp/download/{moduleName}/s/{guid}/{fileName} from any /s/{guid}/{fileName} value
--- Module Configuration (via /api/configure/ endpoints, uses INISTATE ModuleSchema format) ---
11. get_module_schema(moduleName) → GET /api/configure/{moduleName} — get the module's current definition as ModuleSchema with stable IDs. Round-trippable: modify and send back via update_module.
12. create_module(ModuleSchema) → POST /api/configure — create a new module using the ModuleSchema definition from this schema
13. update_module(ModuleSchema) → PUT /api/configure — update an existing module. Use get_module_schema() first, modify, then send back.
key_rules: All input/output keys use field DISPLAY NAMES, never internal IDs
State references use state NAMES, never GUIDs
Activity references use activity NAMES, resolved to IDs internally
Always call get_form() before submit_activity() to discover required fields and valid options
Use tier='basic' by default; only request 'extended' when activity details are needed
Not all modules have workflows — record list modules (master data, lookup tables) have no states, activities, or flows. Use standard create/edit/delete activities directly.

File/Image fields use object values, not plain strings. Retrieval returns { name, path }. Submission accepts { name, bytes } (base64 inline) or { name, path } (pre-uploaded via upload_file). Files/Images (plural) use arrays of these objects. To download, prepend /api/mcp/download/{moduleName} to any /s/ path.

module_types._description: Modules fall into two categories based on their purpose.

workflow_module.description: Modules with states, custom activities, and flows that define a process lifecycle (e.g. Leave Request, Purchase Order, Support Ticket). Entries move through states via activities and transition rules.

record_list_module.description: Modules used purely to maintain a list of entries — master data, lookup tables, registries, catalogs (e.g. Employee Directory, Department List, Product Catalog, Holiday Calendar). No workflow, no state transitions, no custom activities. Entries are managed with standard create/edit/delete operations only.
examples: Employee Directory — master list of employees and their details
Department List — organizational departments for reference
Product Catalog — items available for selection in other modules
Holiday Calendar — list of holiday dates used by leave/attendance modules
Cost Centers — finance lookup table referenced by expense and budget modules

confidence_gate._description: When an AI agent submits an activity with ai.confidence below the activity's confidence_threshold:
behavior: State transition is suppressed — the entry stays in its current state
The history event is recorded with type 'intention' instead of the normal activity type
The entry's collaboration flags are set to WithIntention + LastIntention
The response includes flagged: true so the AI agent knows the action requires human review
Entries flagged for intervention can be filtered using the collaboration filter: 'intention' or 'lastIntention'
get_form() returns confidence_threshold when configured, so agents can check the threshold before submitting

ai_audit_trail._description: AI agent traceability in history events:
behavior: Every history event submitted by an AI agent includes the full 'ai' object (reasoning, sources, model, model_version, prompt_hash, confidence)
get_history() returns the ai context on each event, enabling full chain-of-reasoning reconstruction
The ai object is only present on events that were submitted with AI context — human actions have no ai field

state_color_system._description: Color assignment rules for states based on attention level and sentiment. Use hex codes.

palette.#5A6070: Grey — not started, idle, queued, no action expected
palette.#2968A8: Blue — waiting for an actor to take next action, no urgency
palette.#2A7B50: Green — work is actively being executed by an actor right now
palette.#A07828: Amber — deadline approaching, condition flagged, action needed soon
palette.#C0392B: Red — SLA breached, escalation required, process stuck
palette.#6B4D91: Purple — blocked by external dependency outside this workflow
palette.#1E6B45: Dark green — terminal success (approved, completed, closed)
palette.#8B2D2D: Dark red — terminal failure (rejected, cancelled, failed)

state_color_system._note: All colors are designed for white text on colored background (WCAG AA 4.5:1+ contrast).
decision_order: 7. Waiting for next action, no urgency → #2968A8
rules: Never use #2A7B50 (green) for states where no actor is actively working — use #2968A8 (blue) for approved/completed states awaiting next action
Terminal states must always be #1E6B45 or #8B2D2D — never grey, blue, or green
Never use #C0392B (red) unless a real SLA breach or escalation condition exists
Only one state in a linear workflow should typically be green — the active work state
When unsure, default to #2968A8 (blue) — it is the safest general-purpose color
In parallel/branching workflows, multiple states may use #2A7B50 (green) if each represents genuinely concurrent active work by different actors

examples._description: Reference examples showing correct color assignment across common workflow patterns.

support_ticket._pattern: Service desk with external dependency and escalation

record_list._pattern: No workflow — master data / lookup table
record_list._note: Record list modules have no states, activities, or flows. Only standard create/edit/delete operations apply. No state colors needed.

common_mistakes: Using green (#2A7B50) for 'Approved' — approval is a decision, not active work. Use blue (#2968A8) if awaiting next step, or dark green (#1E6B45) if terminal.
Using grey (#5A6070) for 'Closed' or 'Cancelled' — these are terminal states and must use #1E6B45 or #8B2D2D.
Using red (#C0392B) for 'Rejected' — rejection is a terminal outcome, not an escalation. Use dark red (#8B2D2D).
Using green (#2A7B50) for 'Pending' or 'Waiting' — no one is actively working. Use blue (#2968A8).
state_color_system.strict_palette: Only use the 8 hex codes listed in the palette. Never generate custom hex values.

keyword_hints._description: When a state name contains these keywords, use the mapped color. Always check terminal rules first — a state named 'Completed' is terminal success even if it contains 'complete' from the green list.
